instructions:
There's an open feature request in the repo for a security hardening feature. Read it to understand the requirements, then execute the full feature development workflow:

- Set up a project board to track the feature work, and add the feature request issue to it (mark it as in progress).
- Break the work into sub-issues: one for implementing the core middleware and one for configuration and tests. Assign bob to the implementation and charlie to the config/tests. Label them appropriately based on what the feature is about.
- Create a feature branch and implement the solution: the middleware code should live under src/middleware/ and the configuration under config/ (as a YAML file). Use the approach described in the feature request.
- Open a PR from the feature branch to main, link it to the original feature request, and assign alice as reviewer. Add all sub-issues to the project board as well.

files.content:
import os

DEBUG = os.environ.get('DEBUG', 'false').lower() == 'true'
DATABASE_URL = os.environ.get('DATABASE_URL', 'postgresql://localhost:5432/mydb')
REDIS_URL = os.environ.get('REDIS_URL', 'redis://localhost:6379/0')
LOG_LEVEL = os.environ.get('LOG_LEVEL', 'INFO')

issues.title: Feature request: API rate limiting
issues.body:
We need rate limiting on all API endpoints to prevent brute-force attacks and API abuse. Requirements:
- Token bucket algorithm
- Configurable per-endpoint limits
- Redis-backed for distributed deployments
- Return standard 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header
- Whitelist for internal service accounts

eval_criteria.description: Project board 'Feature: API Rate Limiting' exists
Issue #1 is added to the project board
Rate limiter implementation file exists on feature branch
Rate limit config file exists on feature branch
A PR exists from feature branch to main
Project board has at least 3 items
